programme.title: Baywatch - Baywatch Ohana

programme.title: Baywatch - Stone Cold

programme.title: Baywatch - Broken Promises
programme.desc: "Broken Promises" - To avoid jail, Danny joins Team Baywatch. Leigh doesn't trust him. Kekoa battles her father over her lifeguard career and moves out. JD, Zack and Jason hustle two gorgeous women who turn out to be con artists who turn the tables on them.

programme.title: Baywatch - Dream Girl
programme.desc: "Dream Girl" - Jenna convinces ruthless Councilman McKenna to close the Training Center. Sean suspends Zack after he severely injures JD during a rescue. Kekoa expresses her deep love for JD – but to Leigh! Zack sees a ghostly woman from his dreams.

programme.title: Baywatch - The Cage
programme.desc: "The Cage" - Sean confronts a "shark tour" operator dangerously dumping chum to lure sharks. A tourist who wants to be a hero to impress his son keeps interfering with Jason's rescues. JD and Kekoa try to keep their passionate relationship a secret.

programme.title: Baywatch - Father Faust

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Chapel of Love
programme.desc: "Chapel of Love" - On a particularly lonely Valentine's Day, the men of "21 Jump Street" play poker and exchange bad date stories.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - I'm Okay, You Need Work
programme.desc: "I'm Okay, You Need Work" - Hanson investigates allegations that teens are being mistreated in a private rehabilitation program for drug and alcohol abuse.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Orpheus 3.3
programme.desc: "Orpheus 3.3" - An armed gunman takes Hanson and Amy hostage in a supermarket.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Champagne High
programme.desc: "Champagne High" - Hanson, Penhall and Hoffs go undercover to solve a case involving multiple incidents of shoplifting and a stolen Porsche.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Brother Hanson and the Miracle of Renner's Pond
programme.desc: "Brother Hanson and the Miracle of Renner's Pond" - Hanson investigates an arson case involving a book burning and a science teacher that wants to teach creationism.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Raising Marijuana
programme.desc: "Raising Marijuana" - Hoffs goes undercover as a drug dealer's girlfriend but gets a little too close. Meanwhile, Penhall and Ioki pose as "twins" who want to help ship drugs.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Best Years of Your Life
programme.desc: "Best Years of Your Life" - Hanson and Penhall deal with a suicide while investigating a series of home break-ins.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - Cory and Dean Got Married
programme.desc: "Cory and Dean Got Married" - Penhall and Hoffs work on a special detail assignment to transport a juvenile offender.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - School's Out
programme.desc: "School's Out" - Hanson investigates a series of bathroom bombings and the team disperses when the Jump Street Program is suspended for the summer.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street - The Currency We Trade In
programme.desc: "The Currency We Trade In" - Penhall works on a Major Crimes case with his new partner, Fitzgerald. The case involves allegations of child abuse against a public figure.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: When Doves Cry, Pt. 2
programme.desc: Summer's over and the Degrassi kids are back…in droves. A neighbouring high school has been amalgamated with the Junior High and things are a bit of a zoo.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Girls Just Wanna Have Fun
programme.desc: When Spike reveals the identity of the mystery man she's been dating, Emma is shocked. Mr. Simpson - her teacher! Emma claims she’s okay with it, but if that’s true, why is suddenly being so rude to Mr. Simpson?

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Karma Chameleon
programme.desc: Ashley feels like an outcast. Nobody's willing to forget how she treated them at her party last year. Ash decides to take action, swallows her pride and apologizes to everyone. And it works. She boasts to Terri about her success- it’s like last year never happened!

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Secrets & Lies
programme.desc: After a long absence overseas, Ashley's dad is back in town. She's enjoying his company and attention, but something tells her there's a piece of his life that's being kept from her.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Coming of Age
programme.desc: Lacking real family time at home, Jimmy has been spending more and more time at Ashley's house. He and Toby are like brothers and Kate's begun referring to him as her "third kid".

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Rumours and Reputations
programme.desc: Recently diagnosed with a learning disorder, an embarrassed Liberty is ashamed to tell her friends about her tutoring sessions. Emma sees one of Liberty's private sessions and, putting two-and-two together with Liberty's suspicious behaviour, asks J.T. if he thinks something could be going on with Liberty and Mr. Armstrong the math teacher.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Friday Night
programme.desc: When Sean asks Emma what she's doing tonight, Emma's thrilled - that's a date, right? But nervous Sean is unable to muster the courage to arrange the details and Emma misunderstands his hesitancy as rejection.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Wannabe (aka Friends 4Ever)
programme.desc: When Paige decides to start a Spirit Squad at Degrassi, Emma can't believe that the "sexist" sport of cheerleading is being revived. But Manny, who's had years of gymnastics training and is desperate for something to help make her "cool", wants to try out.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Cabaret
programme.desc: Ashley's convinced that she and Terri are going to be the stars of the school talent show, singing her new (and incredibly earnest) ballad. Terri would rather add Paige -- and some fun sparkle -- to the act, but she's not sure how to tell Ashley that.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Under Pressure
programme.desc: Faced with the pressure of passing Grade 7 on his second time around and his mother's newest threat to take him back to his unhappy life up north, Sean is beginning to slip into old, angry patterns.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: Jagged Little Pill
programme.desc: Perfect Ashley is beginning to bore even herself. With their parents away for the weekend, Ashley decides to join Toby in breaking the "one-guest-each" rule and invites Paige and Hazel to join her and Terri.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation: When Doves Cry, Pt. 1
programme.desc: Summer's over and the Degrassi kids are back…in droves. A neighbouring high school has been amalgamated with the Junior High and things are a bit of a zoo.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Lilo
programme.desc: ''Lilo'' - A seven-month-old puppy impresses Brandon with her talents. But in order to secure a new forever home, Lilo will also need to impress her potential adopters’ anxious dog.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Gryffin
programme.desc: ''Gryffin'' - A three-legged Border Collie mix could go home to an eight-year-old girl with dreams of having her very first dog. But before Gryffin can take that journey, he’ll need some time to heal and learn basic obedience.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Bring Your Dog to Work Day
programme.desc: ''Bring Your Dog to Work Day'' - Five rescued shelter dogs learn what it takes to join their future adopters in the workplace.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Ash
programme.desc: ''Ash'' - Brandon finds a beach-loving family for a friendly Terrier mix that’s been rescued from the shelter. But before Ash can experience fun in the sun with his new adopters, Brandon teaches the dog basic obedience and explores his skills on a surf board.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Ruthie
programme.desc: ''Ruthie'' - A “no dogs allowed” policy gets an eight-month-old Collie-Labrador mix evicted from her first home. But with Brandon’s help, Ruthie finds a new place to live, with a loving couple who have been searching for an emotional support dog.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Rocky
programme.desc: ''Rocky'' - A three-year-old chocolate Labrador, that shows a surprising knack for the sport of dock-diving, could be the perfect companion for a single mom who has two energetic young boys.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Maya
programme.desc: ''Maya'' - Brandon rescues a one-year-old Corgi mix that seems like the perfect match for a couple interested in starting a family. But before the dog will be ready for her new home, she’ll need a few lessons in manners.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Highways and Byways
programme.desc: ''Highways and Byways'' - Five rescued shelter dogs will travel by any means necessary to get closer to their new forever families.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Bebe
programme.desc: ''Bebe'' - Brandon rescues a young Pit Bull that shows signs of separation anxiety and a possible history of abuse. But before he can transition this dog to her new forever home, he must gain her trust and train her on the 7 Common Commands.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Lacy
programme.desc: ''Lacy'' - A five-year-old German Shepherd could be the perfect match for a family still mourning the loss of their previous Shepherd. But before Lacy will be ready for her new home, she’ll need to overcome her distrust of other dogs and befriend the family’s current pack of two.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Special Services
programme.desc: ''Special Services'' - Five rescued shelter dogs learn skills that will make a huge difference in their potential adopters' lives.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Millie
programme.desc: ''Millie'' - After rescuing a shy and fearful one-year-old Terrier mix, Brandon calls upon some of the other ranch residents to help Millie overcome her fears and become a better match for a potential adopter.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Bo
programme.desc: ''Bo'' - A Toy Poodle mix could be the perfect match for a young girl who recently overcame a devastating illness. But only time will tell if Brandon can turn this energetic, young pup into a therapy dog.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Echo
programme.desc: ''Echo'' - A disabled veteran reaches out to Brandon for help, after losing his loyal service dog to a sudden illness. But before Brandon will consider an eleven-month-old Doberman Pinscher ready to assist with this call of duty, he’ll need to put the dog through basic training and prepare him for special services.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Leo
programme.desc: ''Leo'' - A young Labrador-Pointer mix is trained for teen sisters who want a mascot for their family’s band. With Eric’s help, this playful pup learns a special way to participate in upcoming shows by saying goodbye to his future fans.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Pixie
programme.desc: ''Pixie'' - A playful Poodle mix could be the perfect match for a single mom with two talented children. But, to make sure that this placement is a success, Eric must teach Pixie some tricks for fitting in with his new family.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Otis
programme.desc: ''Otis'' - A shy Spaniel mix could be the newest addition to a family that has two energetic young boys. But in order for Otis to fit in with his siblings, he must learn the importance of going to bed on cue.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Cheese
programme.desc: ''Cheese'' - A cute Corgi mix hopes to trade in his space at the shelter for a bungalow near the beach with an active, cycling couple. But to make this transition a success, Cheese must be willing to sit back and go along for the ride.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Annie
programme.desc: ''Annie'' - A sweet Terrier mix is matched up with a couple who are mourning the loss of their two previous dogs. Active Annie jumps at the chance to earn a place in their hearts, while simultaneously offering entertainment for their grandchildren.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Lester
programme.desc: ''Lester'' - A two-month-old puppy could be the perfect match for a marketing executive with a passion for skateboarding. But before Lester can ride off into the sunset with his potential adopter, he must complete basic training.

programme.title: Lucky Dog - Rocket
programme.desc: ''Rocket'' - Two off-roading enthusiasts seek a sidekick for their automotive activities. But before Rocket can be confirmed as their perfect puppy passenger, he must prove that he is all revved up about the family’s high-octane lifestyle.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy - "Shaping the Wilderness"
programme.desc: Over millions of years, nature has evolved to deal with the strongest storms on the planet. This episode looks at varying strategies used by plants and animals that live in the hurricane zone and how they cope with the annual onslaught. From robust crocodiles, to fragile flamingos and delicate tropical shrimp, many species have found ingenious ways of dealing with storm devastation. Explore the tropics from the jungle canopy to subterranean caves and dive beneath the coral seas, as this film reveals that hurricanes can be both beneficial and catastrophic to individuals but, at the level of populations, the deadly storms provide new opportunities for life.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - Fighting for the Truth

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - Dying to Be Young

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - The Mourning After

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - Prescription for Change

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - Burning Questions
programme.desc: "Burning Questions" - Recent college grad Robert Terrible is out drinking at a club with friends celebrating. At 3am, he realizes he better call it a night and heads to the nearby garage where he parked his car.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - A Fatal Attraction
programme.desc: "A Fatal Attraction" - It’s an early Sunday morning when a local resident decides to take a short-cut through the church parking lot. Walking past a lone car, the resident can’t believe his eyes.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner - The Things Men Do

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Can magician Darren run the travellator and cross the Big Red Balls? Will Granny decide she doesn't want any government policy advisors knocking at her door and send Dom flying off into the mud? Or will Sophia the 'Professional Apple' from Cornwall beat them all to it and make it through to the Wipeout Zone without getting juiced?

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Today's courageous band of volunteers include a former world class athlete intent on making a comeback, a bin man with big ideas and a kamikaze housewife who literally launches herself at the Big Red Balls, Granny's House and the Ski Lift. t's a close run thing for the title this week with one finalist proving that theImpossible Snowflakes might actually be possible.

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Total Wipeout, the biggest, brashest and most exhausting game show on television, is back for more bangs, bumps, and bruises. This episode sees the return of the finalists from across the series as they compete for the ultimate title, 'Champions of Champions'. They pit themselves against some of the hardest obstacles we've ever witnessed on their journey to get to the Wipeout Zone.

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Richard Hammond dons his tuxedo for a special program honoring the heroes of Total Wipeout's fifth series. The presenter starts with the 'Best new obstacle award' and will be showing the top five, and the winner for each category. Don't miss out on this special episode because surely, it will make you have good laughs!

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: This is the first 'Celebrity Special' of the season. Today, Richard Hammond presents an extraordinary game show in which ten celebrities tackle one of television's most extreme obstacle courses to win 10,000 pounds for charity. Celebrities are Dalton Grant, Ricky Groves, Gail Emms, Charlie Stayt, Roxanne Pallett, Simon Day, Brian Belo, Camilla Dallerup, Nicola McLean and Julie Peasgood.

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Time again for the world of celebrity to take on the world's most outrageous obstacle course in the name of charity, and in the name of slapstick entertainment! Richard Hammond and Amanda Byram watch the likes of Marcus Patric, Peter Duncan, Susie Amy, Chris Rankin, Donna Fraser, Clare Nasir, Neil Ruddock, Melissa Suffield, Lady Sovereign, and Dom try to make it to the Wipeout Zone.

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Crashes, smashes and hilarious mud splashes are on the cards as ten celebrities from the world of sport, music and television take on one of the largest and most extreme obstacle courses in the world. They journey across the globe to the purpose-built Total Wipeout course in Argentina to put their strength, balance and bravery to the test in the hope of winning 10,000 pounds for charity.

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: This is the second 'Celebrity Special' of the season. This time, all of them face 'The Qualifier' and 'The Crash Mountain' before the eliminations begin. Celebrities are Ali Bastian, Andy Akinwolere, Caroline Faraday, Dean Macey, Ian Watkins, James Redmond, Lisa Scott-Lee, Malandra Burrows, Martin Offiah and Tupele Dorgu.

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. Martin Hall goes inside his actual home to reveal how to practice all areas of the game indoors, utilizing household items such as a chair, bottle cap, and ruler. Martin also shares advice from one of golf's top mental coaches."

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal teach drills centered around getting more distance. Learn how Davis Love III trains to increase swing speed. Plus, Martin teaches a technique used by Olympic sprinters that translates into crushing it off the tee."

programme.title: The Big Break
programme.desc: "Players must traverse the flop wall and successfully predict their opponents' outcomes in order to gain immunity as the day begins. Later, a questionable strategy threatens to send one player home. Hosts: Tom Abbott, Paige Mackenzie. From Barefoot Resort and Golf in Myrtle Beach, SC."

programme.title: The Big Break
programme.desc: "In the season premiere of Golf Channel's flagship reality show, 12 contestants square off in the glass break competition with bragging rights and immunity on the line. From PGA National Resort and Spa in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida."

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. Martin Hall reveals how one piece of wood can help break bad swing habits. Learn how to start the backswing like Bryson DeChambeau and develop extension through the ball like Jack Nicklaus with the use of a two-by-four."

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. Martin Hall and GOLF social media correspondent Nikki B reveal how the average amateur can practice like the best players in the world. Learn why Padraig Harrington does the Happy Gilmore drill and how Ernie Els used a sponge ball to develop his big easy swing. Also, performance coach Iain Highfield teaches the proper way to think and practice to improve the game."

programme.title: The Big Break
programme.desc: "The remaining contestants get creative and look to earn immunity by showing who's game is in the best shape. Some find out they may need more work than they thought. From PGA National Resort and Spa in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida."

programme.title: The Big Break
programme.desc: "The four remaining players vie for an advantage which will go a long way toward determining who will play in the championship match. Head-to-head competitions put a spotlight on the pressure faced in the season's penultimate episode. Hosts: Tom Abbott, Paige Mackenzie. From the Barefoot Resort and Golf in Myrtle Beach, SC."

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal discuss the role of the hands and how to use them through all parts of the swing. Learn what Tom Watson says is his number one swing key, and how household items such as a coat hanger can improve ball striking."

programme.title: School Of Golf (Instructional)
programme.desc: "Instructor Martin Hall provides tips and drills utilizing an in-studio golf simulator. The subject is golf. The teacher is Martin Hall. Each week the assignment is to improve your game. Along with co-host Nikki B, Martin covers everything from tee to green while answering viewer questions. Get your homework assignment to improve your game."

programme.title: The Big Break

programme.title: Ford's Fishing Frontiers: South Dakota Trout
programme.desc: For 30 years, Preston Sharp and Jim Whitlock, have shared South Dakota's black hills fishing. In that time, White Wood Creek has been resurrected, from a casualty of the mining industry, to spawning grounds for many species of trout. An old mine that once produced gold and pollution, now purifies the waters of the creek and enriches the surrounding eco-system, and once again, has provided the opportunity to explore the backcountry to find wild mountain trout - see what lies ahead on Ford's Fishing Frontiers.

programme.title: Zona's Awesome Fishing Show: Zona Solo - Lake St. Clair

programme.title: The Musky Hunter: Afternoon Delight
programme.desc: There’s nothing like spending an afternoon on the water. To maximize your time, sometimes it’s best to troll and other times it’s best to cast. Regardless, you need a strategy as you transition from daylight into evening. First, Jim Saric and musky hunter Charlie Buhler troll in early summer. Then, Jim and Pro Guide Mike Hulbert cast Lake St. Clair.

programme.title: Lake Commandos: Pennaz vs. Elite Pro

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV: Pick Your Pocket Presentations

programme.title: Fishful Thinker: Lure Development Details
programme.desc: Ever wonder about the process of developing new lures and bringing them to international markets? Host Chad LaChance did so he invited the lure develop manager from Berkley, Dan Spengler to be a guest on FTTV. The two pounded largemouth and smallmouth bass while talking lure details.

programme.title: Federal Premium's Black Cloud: Last Go
programme.desc: A bonus bag of Green

programme.title: Ducks Unlimited TV: Legacy of Conservation
programme.desc: This week Doug Larsen heads to the Land of Lincoln, Illinois. Joining him is DU’s Director of Development for the state, James Roberts, and a cast of DU volunteers chasing ducks across public and private landsHunters realized long ago that this confluence of rivers and wetlands teemed with waterfowl, offering some of the best hunting in Illinois. Larsen closes out the hunt with Mark Staley and sons. The Staley family has a long DU history going all the way back to the founding in 1937. This week on DUTV!

programme.title: Sporting Dog Adventures: Alberta Beat Down
programme.desc: Jeff Fuller, Rod Haydel of Haydel's Game Calls and the crew finish up their world-class waterfowl hunt in Alberta.

programme.title: Wingshooting USA: Manny's Redux
programme.desc: Our “Take Your Friend Hunting” sweepstakes winners share their first South Dakota pheasant hunt at Hitchcock’s Ringneck Retreat. Hot, dry conditions challenge dogs and hunters, and Scott’s younger wirehair Manny tries to re-create his wintry debut of a few years ago, running the same fields but with quite different results. Darik Tschetter’s shorthair, wirehair and Lab produce in every conceivable habitat.

programme.title: Swarovski Optik Quests: Wyoming Monster Mule Deer
programme.desc: Spot and stalk Monster Mule deer in Wyoming with the Swarovski Optik Sales force.

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Hunting Adventures: Pacific Rim Guide Outfitters Best Of

programme.title: Day One Outdoors: Buoy 10 - Opening Day
programme.desc: The Super Bowl of Salmon Fishing takes place at the mouth of the Columbia River near one of the most Dangerous Bar Crossings in the World! Watch as Anglers find the fish in this 40 mile area on the August 1st opener!

programme.title: Innerlocs Outthere - Innerlocs Outthere - "Brokey"
programme.desc: Justin Merkel has been hunting a specific piece of property his entire life. He has a choice to make on a buck he knows so well. Will Brokey get a free pass or will the property go up for sale. Its a Tennessee showdown with a bittersweet ending.

programme.title: Team200 - Team200 - "The Perfect Storm for Giant Whitetails"
programme.desc: Is there a “Perfect Storm” for killing the biggest bucks in your area?

programme.title: Full Strut - Full Strut - "Level Playing Field"
programme.desc: Brandon tends to be behind the camera most of the time filming Mike across the country, After Mike having a very successful trip to Kansas he surprises Brandon with being the first up to bat to get after his first ever Rio! After things starting off pretty tough they make a decision to level the playing field!

programme.title: Small Town Hunting - Small Town Hunting - "Missouri Deer Opener"
programme.desc: Opening weekend of rifle season in Missouri and we are back with Missouri Hunting and Fishing Adventures looking for a couple bucks to play the game!

programme.title: Storytellers - Storytellers - "Run-N-Gun Adventures"
programme.desc: The Texas Gulf Coast offers an array of opportunities to experience the outdoors. From fishing to waterfowl, we have found that every hunt gives us a chance to experience other people's stories. On this hunt, we learned not only about the incredible adventures that take place at Run-N-Gun, but also about a Texas based organization changing the lives of young people.

programme.title: Full Strut - Full Strut - "Level Playing Field"

programme.desc: As hunters, we need to understand the why behind our hunt.

programme.title: Blood Origins - Blood Origins - "Family: Kim Rogers"
programme.desc: You make you a hunter.

programme.title: Blood Origins - Blood Origins - "Perspectives: Margit Axelrad"
programme.desc: As hunters, we need to fully understand why we hunt & hear the perspectives of non-hunters as well.

programme.title: The Raven Project - The Raven Project - "Last Ditch Effort"
programme.desc: With everything breaking down Dustin and Randy head to an auction to hopefully save their weekend plans of getting seed into the ground.

programme.title: Local Lines - Local Lines - "How It All Started"
programme.desc: In this episode Captain Brandon Branch decided to go back to his roots and fish with a long time friend Laremy Strickland. They've been fishing together for over 10 years now and on this episode they'll be competing to see who can catch the biggest bag.

programme.title: Modern Fishing - Modern Fishing - "Panamanian Fishing Paradise Part 1"
programme.desc: Jared returns to one of his beloved fishing destinations Panama. Jared managed to run into a massive yellowfin bait ball on his last trip, so hopes are high for this year's haul. Though it's slow goings at the start, the crew finds out that Panama always delivers on the yellowfin

programme.title: Guiding Flow - Guiding Flow - "Critical Migrations: Menhaden"
programme.desc: Capt. Benny Blanco and Capt. Brandon Cyr travel from Miami to Boston to understand the massive importance of a healthy menhaden fishery. They target tarpon, bonefish, striped bass, and tuna along the way

programme.title: Stomping Grounds - Stomping Grounds - "Fishing Florida's Backcountry Wilderness"
programme.desc: Host Ryan McVinney takes Boat Trader fans on an indepth exploration of Florida flats fishing deep in the backcountry of Islamorada. Featuring Captain Eric Lund, a Wall Street Broker turned backcountry fishing guide, this episode reveals the key design features of the world’s best flats skiffs and how they are built for targeting some of the most exciting sportfish species on the planet. Also, covered are bay boats and their versatility for families exploring Florida’s coastal waterways, along with a detailed study of fish species and wildlife in the region. Some of the area’s most beautiful and secret locales show why Islamorada has earned the nickname of the Sportfish Capital of The World.

programme.title: Reel Life Adventures - Reel Life Adventures - "Dry Tortugas"
programme.desc: Tucker and JC Carlson Tucker and JC Carlson explore the Dry Tortugas and Fort Jefferson out of Key West in their brand new Yellowfin center console. The boys learn about Florida Keys history and experience red hot fishing for permit, cobia and more.

programme.title: Gale Force Twins - Gale Force Twins - "Our Final Catch Fishing For 4 Days And 3 Nights"
programme.desc: It's the FINAL morning. The sun hasn't risen yet but the mates are hard at work transferring the fish into bins in order to properly sort and distribute it to the anglers. As the sun begins to rise the anglers gather around with their coolers ready to collect their fish! After the coolers are full we say our goodbyes and head home but the work is NOT over yet....we still have to fillet our catch!

programme.title: The Chefs’ Line - French

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares - J. Willy's
programme.desc: Located in Indiana, J. Willy's Bar and Grille is deep in debt. When Chef Ramsay arrives, he challenges the owners to use fresh ingredients, but the inexperienced chefs get overwhelmed.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares - Café 36
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay drops by Café 36, a French-American eatery in Illinois. The couple who own the place are lacking in business skills and need Ramsay's expertise to teach them how to draw in a crowd.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen - 4 Chefs Compete
programme.desc: The remaining chefs participate in the "taste it, now make it" challenge. The winner dines out with guests the losers move furniture and prep the kitchen for dinner service.

programme.title: Big Appetite
programme.desc: Ben Hundreds spins through his hometown of L.A. visiting Chifa, a fashion-infused Peruvian/Chinese restaurant, authentic Mexican at La Casita Mexicana and Motherwolf - Evan Funke’s opulent Roman spot. Plus art galleries, shopping and burgers.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's London
programme.desc: Rachel goes for a decadent champagne afternoon tea in Mayfair and makes some finger-licking ribs.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's London
programme.desc: Rachel celebrates London's love with the East as she makes sticky chicken with Malaysian salad and soft steamed buns.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's London
programme.desc: In this episode all about lazy days, Rachel cycles along a canal, visits her friend Tom's urban garden, and whips up a "shepherdless pie".

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's London
programme.desc: Rachel visits a basement Norwegian style salmon smokehouse and a shop where the shelves are stocked with preserves made on a hob at home.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's London
programme.desc: Rachel celebrates Londoners' love of tradition as she puts her own twist on the traditional fry-up and makes a simple one-tray roast.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Who said struggle meals can’t be delicious? Don’t sleep on these 5-ingredient recipes – simple weeknight meals that are anything but boring, and with a few pantry staples, you’ll have dinner on the table in no time.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie teaches us a few easy and inventive “no-kitchen” hacks for delicious struggly meals. If you're dorm living or road tripping, it doesn’t mean you can’t make tasty meals from handy kitchen (and non-kitchen) appliances. No Kitchen? No problem.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: No veggie family packs the same nutritious punch as the cruciferous family, and with a shelf life this long, they’ve become a fixture in Frankie’s meal rotation. These delicious and easy-to-make recipes will win everyone over in no time.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: There may be infinite ways to define pie, but the only math that matters is what makes these sweet and savory dishes fit the bill. In true Struggle Style, Frankie rolls out his best tips and tricks for enjoying hassle free, cost friendly pies.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: It’s the dead of winter in Frankie’s kitchen, which means it’s that time for hearty, delicious, and warming homemade meals. So you’re saving more money, while you’re staying in, already saving money. It’s a winter miracle.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Cabbage has long been an underrated green on the field, and it’s about time Frankie gives it a starring role. This leafy head proves itself as the versatile ingredient we need it to be, affordable year around, delicious and filling.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Give your wallet a break by taking the fine-dining experience straight to the comfort of your home. From tender, juicy steak paired with classic sides, to decadent desserts, Frankie’s got your date night meal covered.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie teaches us how to use whole vegetables, from root-to-stem, which means no food goes to waste. Learn how to use vegetable stalks you would normally throw away. Root-to-stem cooking: less waste, more food, and more nutrition. Frankie’s favorite

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Cheese can be really expensive. Frankie teaches us how to cook with more affordable cheese swaps, and we learn how to stretch cheese in a dish so it can be the star without breaking the bank. Bonus: Frankie teaches how to make cheese at home!

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Amp up your salad game with a little mixing and matching, from light and leafy greens to crisp and hearty toppings, and dressings for all occasions. Frankie shows us how versatile and fulfilling a homemade salad can truly be.

programme.title: Frankie vs. the Internet
programme.desc: Baking videos send our social media feeds in a frenzy and we can’t seem to get enough. Watch as creators impress the judges with a show stopping focaccia, then pay tribute to the mini pancake cereal trend by concocting their own unique cereal.

programme.desc: Expert chef and home cook swap their prized recipes and ingredients for your culinary viewing pleasure!

programme.desc: We partnered with expert food makers to demonstrate and describe their craft in exhaustive detail.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: The humble tomato - called the pomodoro, or 'apple of gold' in Italy.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Amanda prepares three summer dishes using raspberries, mint, peas, and apricots.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Amanda prepares dishes featuring collard greens, goat cheddar, heirloom cherry tomatoes and maple syrup.

programme.title: Road Grill

programme.title: Road Grill

programme.title: Fish the Dish

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts fuses seafood and booze together.

programme.title: License to Grill
programme.desc: Chef Rob Rainford prepares veal, grilled onions, asparagus rafts, and scallops.

programme.title: License to Grill
programme.desc: Chef Rob Rainford creates an Asian Feast including rotisserie duck, seared tuna, and smoked veggies.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: Chef Michael Allemeier embraces his wild side and demonstrates great tips and delicious recipes for cooking wild game.

programme.title: Barbecued Salmon
programme.desc: We were intrigued with the notion of barbecuing a whole side of salmon. Our goal was to have this large piece of fish spend enough time over the coals to pick up some real wood flavor. A bright cucumber salad adds cool, tart, and creamy flavors to complement the smoke-flavored salmon.

programme.title: Hearty Soups
programme.desc: On a cold day, few dishes are as comforting as a hearty soup-unless it turns out starchy or gluey. Watch as we make thick and creamy versions of two of our favorites: ham and split pea, and rustic potato-leek.

programme.title: Steak and Potatoes
programme.desc: Purchase a good steak and cook it properly, and all it needs is salt and pepper-and, of course, a side of spuds. Unfortunately, many of the inexpensive steaks labeled "great for grilling'' at the supermarket turn out tough and dry with little flavor. And mashed potatoes are often gluey or runny. We wanted potatoes with perfect texture and big flavor-something unusual to accompany a great steak and a good bottle of wine.

programme.title: Regional Classics
programme.desc: Some local dishes become so popular that they transcend their region. Gumbo is a taste of Louisiana in a big old bowl. And Cincinnati chili is American original, served with buttered spaghetti, kidney beans, cheddar cheese, and chopped onions. Our goal when developing our versions of these dishes was to remain faithful to the originals while making the recipes approachable for cooks who may not have grown up with them.

programme.title: Four-Star Desserts
programme.desc: Watch as we take chocolate volcano cake and caramelized pears and make them approachable for the home cook.

programme.title: Let's Talk Turkey
programme.desc: Thanksgiving can strike fear into the heart of even the most proficient cook. First, there's the turkey to contend with-in no other meal is the main course paraded in front of guests with as much fanfare. Sweet potato casserole is often considered another must-have. With our recipes for these Thanksgiving standards, we minimize the work so that the cook can enjoy the holiday as much as everyone else.

programme.title: Old-Fashioned Chocolate Cake
programme.desc: Our goals in this episode are twofold: create an old-fashioned chocolate layer cake with the same moist, tender crumb we so fondly remember from our childhoods, and infuse it with true chocolate flavor-chocolaty enough to please adults, but not so dense that it might scare away the kids at the table.

programme.title: Beer Can Chicken Dinner
programme.desc: In this episode, we show how a few easy tricks can give an old standby, like roast chicken, a new lift.

programme.title: Chocolate Delights
programme.desc: Bridget and Julia uncover the secrets to making Foolproof All-Butter Pie Dough and Chocolate Cream Pie, and tasting expert Jack Bishop challenges Bridget to a tasting of cocoa powder. Finally, test cook Elle Simone makes Julia a decadent Dark Chocolate Fudge Sauce.

programme.title: The Perfect Cake
programme.desc: Bridget and Julia make the perfect Gingerbread Layer Cake and Keith Makes Sticky Toffee Pudding Cakes.

programme.title: Elegant Dinner Party
programme.desc: In this episode, Bridget and Julia uncover the secrets to the ultimate Braised Brisket with Pomegranate, Cumin, and Cilantro. In the Equipment Corner, equipment expert Adam Ried reviews his top pick for twist corkscrews. Finally, test cook Dan Souza makes a decadent Duchess Potato Casserole.

programme.title: Holiday Feast
programme.desc: Test cook Ashley Moore shows host Bridget Lancaster how to make a Crumb-Crusted Rack of Lamb that is perfect for the holidays. Next, equipment expert Adam Ried shows host Julia Collin Davison his top picks of electric knives. Finally, test cook Christie Morrison shows Julia how to make a showstopping Brussels Sprout Gratin.

programme.title: Pub-Style Favorites, Revisited
programme.desc: Hosts Julia Collin Davison and Bridget Lancaster uncover the secrets to making the ultimate Pub-Style Steak and Ale Pie at home. Then, equipment expert Adam Ried reveals his top pick for knife blocks. Finally, test cook Keith Dresser makes Bridget perfect Boston Brown Bread.

programme.title: How to Roast Everything
programme.desc: Bridget and Julia teach viewers how to make the ultimate Beef Tenderloin with Smoky Potatoes and Persillade Relish. Tasting expert Jack Bishop challenges Bridget to a turkey tasting. Gadget critic Lisa McManus reviews fat separators, and then test cook Dan Souza makes Turkey Breast en Cocotte with Pan Gravy.

programme.title: Comfort Food Done Right
programme.desc: Test cook Christie Morrison and host Bridget Lancaster uncover the secrets to making a flavorful Double-Crust Chicken Pot Pie from scratch. Next, tasting expert Jack Bishop challenges host Julia Collin Davison to a tasting of creamy peanut butter. Finally, test cook Ashley Moore makes Julia a delicious First Lady specialty, Cowboy Cookies.

programme.title: Roast Beef and Potatoes
programme.desc: Test cook Natalie Estrada reveals the secrets to making a foolproof Classic Roast Beef Tenderloin with host Bridget Lancaster. Next, host Julia Collin Davison discovers equipment expert Adam Ried's top pick of tongs. Finally, test cook Lawman Johnson shows Julia how to make perfect Lyonnaise Potatoes.

programme.title: Italian Comfort Food Classics
programme.desc: Test cook Bryan Roof cooks host Julia Collin Davison a surprising Italian classic: Chicago Thin-Crust Pizza with Homemade Italian Sausage. Next, tasting expert Jack Bishop challenges host Bridget Lancaster to a live taste test of jarred pasta sauce. Finally, test cook Lawman Johnson and host Bridget reveal the secrets to a perfect Pasta Fagioli.

programme.title: Lidia's Italian Table - Bistecca Fiorentina
programme.desc: Italians are not big beef eaters. When they do indulge, it is rarely with steak…except in Florence, where Bistecca Fiorentina is a favorite. At 2” thick, grilled over coals, with a very special anchovy, rosemary and salt rub, it is served with braised cannellini beans. Caramelized tomatoes (yes!) over vanilla ice cream for dessert it’s a rich and robust dessert after Bistecca!

programme.title: Lidia's Italian Table - Lasagna & Caesar Salad
programme.desc: Caesar Salad is a staple on the Italian-American table, even though it’s origin in Mexican. Lidia’s version is the best of both, and shows us why it is probably America’s most popular salad. And who doesn’t love Lasagna? Lidia’s is classic Italian-American Lassgna steeped in her special meat sauce.

programme.title: Ciao Italia - The King of Cheese
programme.desc: The crowned king of cheese is featured when food educator Nancy Radke joins Mary Ann nella cucina to create a table filled with delicious antipasti featuring Parmigiano Reggiano cheese and Prosciutto di Parma ham, starting with Prosciutto di Parma Wrapped Fruit with Parmigiano Reggiano, Parmesan Cheese and Almond Wafers, and Spicy Fava Bean Spread.

programme.title: Ciao Italia - Grandmother’s Meatballs
programme.desc: Mary Ann’s in a meatball mood and creates Polpette Toscane (Tuscan Meatballs) that calls for meat, mushrooms, parsley and – surprise - deep-frying. Then good friend, Lulu San Angelone, stops by to create Polpettine Della Nonna (Grandmother’s meatballs) which are baked, not fried. See old familiar friends in a brand new way when two these two great cooks get together.

programme.title: Ciao Italia - Fish Stew
programme.desc: Mary Ann visits good friend and great cook, Maria Pia in the land of the leaning tower - Pisa, Italy. Once in her kitchen, red mullet are sent swimming into a spicy tomato sauce. Hooked on seafood, Mary Ann goes nella cucina to create Caccuccio alla Livornese a fish stew filled with so much swordfish, shrimp, scallops, monkish and squid, there’s hardly room for the herb-filled, tomato sauce.

programme.title: Ciao Italia - Amarena Cherry Roll
programme.desc: There are cakes and there are CAKES. Paul and Anna Ciramitaro of Caffe Sicilia in Gloucester, Massachusetts, prepare an eye-popping Cassata guaranteed to satisfy the sweetest of sweet teeth. But just in case it doesn’t, Mary Ann creates a pistachio pudding-filled Amarena Cake just to be sure you’ll shout "basta!” (enough!)

programme.title: Ciao Italia - Dumplings to Die For
programme.desc: Mary Ann features Canederli with Speck, a fantastic dumpling-like recipe from the little-known Italian region of Trentino Aldo Adige. Filled with diced speck, this unique meat product adds an unmistakable, smoke-like flavor to the tender canederli, bobbing in broth. Perfect for a cold winter’s night.

programme.title: Michael Chiarello's Napa - Dinner Duet
programme.desc: Some foods are sexy because of their texture or flavor, and some are sexy because of how they're served. These are the foods we'd cook for a very important evening. A lush seafood stew with scallops, shrimp, mussels, clams and Pernod, a whole-leaf Caesar salad made to be eaten with your fingers, and a silken Caramel Panna Cotta. We'll visit Greystone, one of the oldest buildings in the valley.

programme.title: Michael Chiarello's Napa - Late Night Dining
programme.desc: We take you along to the biggest charity event in the Napa Valley and then come back home to make some late-night favorites: a home-style onion soup, a spicy spaghetti Amatriciana, and dried-fruit compote flavored with Sambuca.

programme.title: Michael Chiarello's Napa - Winter Favorites
programme.desc: When the temperature drops, foods should deepen in flavor. These are the dishes we make when it's cold outside. A Chicken Bolognaise that's lower in fat than the traditional version, Fennel Roasted Vegetables, and a cheesecake made with flavorful mascarpone.

programme.title: Michael Chiarello's Napa - Pesto Perfecto
programme.desc: Pesto is much more than basil and pine nuts. Learn the technique and make tomato pesto (with our infamous flowerpot chicken and bruschetta), walnut pesto and even your own pesto with the flavors you choose. Meet some olive growers who make olive oil in the Napa Valley.

programme.title: Michael Chiarello's Napa - Panzanella
programme.desc: Italians make this bread and vegetable salad in the summer with fresh tomatoes and garlic. We show how to make it all year long, with a springtime asparagus panzanella, a classic summer version, and with veal medallions to serve as a main course. We'll tour Main Street, St. Helena, and see some of the valley's most charming stores and restaurants.

programme.title: Taste of France - Pascal Barbot - Astrance
programme.desc: In today’s program we will visit the star-chef Pascal Barbot, who became independent at age twenty-seven. Reservations at his 2 star Michelin-awarded restaurant Astrance are booked months ahead. Pascal is truly passionate about cooking and puts emphasis on perfection in everything that he and his team does. He loves surprises, and also offers his customers this delight, with his surprise menu. In this program we’ll get to know Barbot and the values that permeate his work and relation with his producers and learn his secrets on how to cook like a star!

programme.title: Taste of France - Daniel Barratier – L’Auberge Sur-les-Bois
programme.desc: Daniel Barratier has worked with some of the best chefs traveling the world, now he’s back in his hometown, Annecy, the ”Venice of the French Alps" were he invested in a true fine dining restaurant. We’ll explore his life philosophy and also meet with his favorite pig farmer that started a trend that will change France forever.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen - Bacon or Pancetta
programme.desc: Chef Stellino attempts to answer the age old question - which is better - bacon or pancetta? Dishes include: Ragu' Di Gamberetti/ Shrimp Amatriciana with Pasta / Insalata Del Sogno / Watermelon Salad with Tomato and Bacon.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen - Family Matters
programme.desc: Chef Stellino recalls the charming quirks of his family in Italy and America. Dishes Include: Pasta All' Arrabbiata (Pasta with a spicy tomato sauce) / Salsicce Brasate con Broccoli e Peperoni (Braised sausages with broccoli and peppers) / Banana Cream Pudding.

programme.title: MIX - Old Favorites & Winery Tour
programme.desc: Tamee visits four of her favorite bars from seasons past to mix up more incredible cocktails.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in his home town of Saigon just in time for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish topped with grilled pork and herbs.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues through the streets of Saigon visiting his favourite Pho restaurant, then it’s off to Miss YungJow, who has been cooking a different dish every day for twenty years. In District 4, Luke cooks up some spicy Chilli Crab Claws.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food journey venturing into Bangkok, Thailand, with different street food vendors on every corner. He learns how to cook a traditional Thai dessert, Thai Coconut Pudding and queues up for the best Thai Red Pork in town.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke searches for the best Tom Yum soup in Bangkok and entertains the locals with a rare Thai delicacy, dancing shrimp salad. He wanders through the streets of Chinatown and dines at a 70 year old street food stall millimetres away from traffic.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: In multicultural Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, Luke meets his friend Tana in Brickfields, filled with a variety of different Indian dishes then meets the famous Mr Hung with a reputation for flying instant noodles nearly two stories high.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke starts his day at the central Jalan Imbi market in Kuala Lumpur with a street food medley for breakfast. Later, he meets Mr Sou who has been cooking a hearty Pork Bone Soup with medicinal herbal properties for over 20 years.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food adventure exploring Jakarta, Indonesia. Meeting up with his old friend, Derice, Luke unearths the delicious delicacies of Chinatown, finding the experience a melting pot of diverse cultural cuisines.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke completes his street food journey in Jakarta, Indonesia where he visits a busy stall that’s been serving a traditional Indonesian salad since 1961. Luke indulges in an Indonesian dessert, the sweet Martabak, known as the ‘King of Street Food’.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke discovers the unique flavours and culinary traditions of the Bai and Yai people in the historic towns of Dali and Shaxi in China's Yunnan Province.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke reaches the fabled town of Lijiang in China's Yunnan Province, where the nightlife reigns and a Naxi "aunty" teaches Luke how to cook a local speciality.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke journeys to Xishuangbana, where China meets Southeast Asia, to experience Dai culture and cuisines, and try the acquired tastes of the famous tea horse trail.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke discovers the little known foods and flavours of Yangon – the former capital city of Myanmar.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke ventures to the Shan State, where entire villages and kilometres of tomato farms float upon Inle Lake.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke hikes to the hill tribe villages of the Shan State and the township of Keng Tung, where he cooks some unusual delicacies and local marketplace favourites.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke makes it to the town of Chiang Khong in northern Thailand, where he literally catches the ingredients for his traditional Tom Yum fish soup and tasty northern-style frog curry.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke arrives at mountainous Mae Salong, where he prepares a rustic tomato dish with Apear, an Aka grandmother.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Greater Mekong
programme.desc: Luke stays in Luang Prabang to join the biggest New Years celebrations in the country.

programme.title: Holidays Unpacked S01 E03 - Cape Verde & The Azores
programme.desc: Lucy Hedges visits Cape Verde, where she relaxes in the year round sunshine before heading out from the golden beaches to sail the blue ocean waters and dive over one of over 70 shipwrecks on the Islands. The Azores are known as "Europe's answer to Hawaii" and Morland experiences the wild side of these volcanic islands.

programme.title: Holidays Unpacked S01 E04 - Cambodia & Iceland
programme.desc: Lucy Hedges visits Cambodia, the up and coming destination of the Far East. Morland is in Iceland visiting the dramatic backdrops to some of Hollywood's biggest movies.

programme.title: Here I Come - Clearwater
programme.desc: Come and experience Clearwater's beautiful beaches and meet the worlds most famous celebrity dolphin. Take a leap of faith and fuel your adrenaline while zip lining through the trees. Feast on the culture and culinary diversity of St. Petes, all while making friends along the way!

programme.title: Here I Come - Tarpon Springs
programme.desc: Take a trip to Tarpon Springs, Florida's sponge diving capital. Play ball with Willy Adames of the Tampa Bay Rays. Fire up your artistic flare at the Morean glass studio and finally Swing-dance the night away at the historical Gulf Port Casino!

programme.title: Havana Curveball
programme.desc: Mica, is a classic young teen. Enthusiastic. Idealistic. Dreaming baseball. At 13, he is studying for his Bar Mitzvah, the Jewish coming of age rite. An earnest kid, he takes to heart his Rabbi's requirement to help "heal the world." Imagining himself a savior of sorts, he launches a grand plan to send baseballs to Cuba, a country with a mysterious pull. He knows only that Cubans are poor and love baseball-and that Cuba saved his grandpa's life. On a hunch, his award-winning filmmaker parents pick up their camera. They know the U.S. embargo with Cuba-and the complications of growing up-will throw him a curveball.

programme.title: Great Wide Open
programme.desc: A celebration of America's National Parks and the incredible adventurers who explore them.

programme.title: Kung Fu Motion - Hip Hop Stilts
programme.desc: Harry doesn't know how to stilt walk but within 5 days he will have to crash course the basics and advance to hip-hop stilt-walking in order to perform to a critical audience in China.

programme.title: Kung Fu Motion - Rolling Lamp
programme.desc: Putting to the test all his muscles including scalp and tongue, Harry must balance a bowl of burning oil on his head while making a mockery of himself in a Sichuan opera.

programme.title: Kung Fu Motion - Lion Dance
programme.desc: Harry has to hold a horse stance, move like a cat and capture the spirit of the lion before he is allowed to perform Lion Dancing at a troupe's 190th anniversary celebrations.

programme.title: Kung Fu Motion - Man Juggling
programme.desc: Harry joins the Shandong Acrobatic Troupe famous for juggling with their feet fellow team mates to form a towering pyramid of men.

programme.title: Building the Dream - Peak District
programme.desc: Charlie comes to the aid of architect Matthew Lewis, who wants to build the perfect family home in an extraordinary site on the edge of the Peak District.

programme.title: Building the Dream - Stirlingshire
